gaps.title: Round-robin counter state loss on restart
gaps.breaks: Ticket distribution resumes from wrong position after restart, causing uneven workload
gaps.scenario: System processes 100 tickets with round_robin, restarts at ticket 50, but counter resets to 0, causing first variant to receive tickets 50-100 while others remain idle
gaps.prompt: Write a test that simulates a Celery worker restart during round-robin ticket processing, verifies counter state persists correctly across restarts, and confirms workload distribution remains even

gaps.title: Least-loaded capacity check bypass
gaps.breaks: Tickets assigned to overloaded variants when capacity checks fail
gaps.scenario: Variant A has 1000/1000 tickets but appears as 0/1000 due to Redis cache miss, causing new tickets to be assigned to already overloaded variant
gaps.prompt: Write a test that simulates Redis cache failure for capacity tracking, verifies fallback mechanism works, and prevents ticket assignment to overloaded variants

gaps.title: Channel_pinned fallback race condition
gaps.breaks: Fallback to other channels during channel failure causes duplicate assignments
gaps.scenario: Primary channel for pinned variant fails during fallback, causing same ticket to be assigned to multiple variants simultaneously
gaps.prompt: Write a test that simulates channel failure during pinned variant assignment, verifies atomic fallback behavior, and prevents duplicate ticket assignments

gaps.title: Rebalance race condition during concurrent rebalancing
gaps.breaks: Tickets double-assigned or lost when multiple rebalance operations run simultaneously
gaps.scenario: Two rebalance operations start at same time, both try to move same ticket from variant A to B, resulting in ticket being assigned to both or lost entirely
gaps.prompt: Write a test that simulates concurrent rebalance operations with shared resources, verifies proper locking mechanisms, and ensures no double assignments or ticket loss

gaps.title: Escalation from highest variant
gaps.breaks: System attempts to escalate beyond highest variant tier
gaps.scenario: Ticket is in High variant (highest tier), system tries to escalate it further, causing error and ticket loss
gaps.prompt: Write a test that verifies escalation logic prevents attempts to escalate beyond the highest variant tier and handles this edge case gracefully

gaps.title: Complete_ticket idempotency with counter underflow
gaps.breaks: Counter goes negative when completing same ticket multiple times
gaps.scenario: complete_ticket called twice for same ticket, causing variant capacity counter to go from 5 to 3 then to 1 instead of staying at 5
gaps.prompt: Write a test that verifies idempotent behavior of complete_ticket operation, especially with concurrent calls, and prevents counter underflow

gaps.title: Orchestration summary accuracy with mixed instance states
gaps.breaks: Summary shows incorrect counts when some instances are in partial states
gaps.scenario: Variant has 100 tickets, 50 completed, 30 in progress, 20 pending - but summary shows 100 pending due to not accounting for in-progress state
gaps.prompt: Write a test that verifies orchestration summary accuracy across different ticket states (pending, in-progress, completed) and accounts for all variants regardless of their state

raw_response:
GAPS FOUND: 7

GAP 1
Severity: CRITICAL
Title: Round-robin counter state loss on restart
What breaks: Ticket distribution resumes from wrong position after restart, causing uneven workload
Real scenario: System processes 100 tickets with round_robin, restarts at ticket 50, but counter resets to 0, causing first variant to receive tickets 50-100 while others remain idle
AI agent prompt: Write a test that simulates a Celery worker restart during round-robin ticket processing, verifies counter state persists correctly across restarts, and confirms workload distribution remains even

GAP 2
Severity: CRITICAL
Title: Least-loaded capacity check bypass
What breaks: Tickets assigned to overloaded variants when capacity checks fail
Real scenario: Variant A has 1000/1000 tickets but appears as 0/1000 due to Redis cache miss, causing new tickets to be assigned to already overloaded variant
AI agent prompt: Write a test that simulates Redis cache failure for capacity tracking, verifies fallback mechanism works, and prevents ticket assignment to overloaded variants

GAP 3
Severity: HIGH
Title: Channel_pinned fallback race condition
What breaks: Fallback to other channels during channel failure causes duplicate assignments
Real scenario: Primary channel for pinned variant fails during fallback, causing same ticket to be assigned to multiple variants simultaneously
AI agent prompt: Write a test that simulates channel failure during pinned variant assignment, verifies atomic fallback behavior, and prevents duplicate ticket assignments

GAP 4
Severity: HIGH
Title: Rebalance race condition during concurrent rebalancing
What breaks: Tickets double-assigned or lost when multiple rebalance operations run simultaneously
Real scenario: Two rebalance operations start at same time, both try to move same ticket from variant A to B, resulting in ticket being assigned to both or lost entirely
AI agent prompt: Write a test that simulates concurrent rebalance operations with shared resources, verifies proper locking mechanisms, and ensures no double assignments or ticket loss

GAP 5
Severity: HIGH
Title: Escalation from highest variant
What breaks: System attempts to escalate beyond highest variant tier
Real scenario: Ticket is in High variant (highest tier), system tries to escalate it further, causing error and ticket loss
AI agent prompt: Write a test that verifies escalation logic prevents attempts to escalate beyond the highest variant tier and handles this edge case gracefully

GAP 6
Severity: MEDIUM
Title: Complete_ticket idempotency with counter underflow
What breaks: Counter goes negative when completing same ticket multiple times
Real scenario: complete_ticket called twice for same ticket, causing variant capacity counter to go from 5 to 3 then to 1 instead of staying at 5
AI agent prompt: Write a test that verifies idempotent behavior of complete_ticket operation, especially with concurrent calls, and prevents counter underflow

GAP 7
Severity: MEDIUM
Title: Orchestration summary accuracy with mixed instance states
What breaks: Summary shows incorrect counts when some instances are in partial states
Real scenario: Variant has 100 tickets, 50 completed, 30 in progress, 20 pending - but summary shows 100 pending due to not accounting for in-progress state
AI agent prompt: Write a test that verifies orchestration summary accuracy across different ticket states (pending, in-progress, completed) and accounts for all variants regardless of their state
